requirement: Principles
General requirements
Usability Engineering Process
The manufacturer shall establish, document, implement and maintain a usability engineering process.

requirement: Risk control as it relates to user interface design

To reduce use-related risk, the manufacturer shall use one or more of the following options, in the order of priority: (a) inherent safety by design (D); (b) protective measures in the medical device itself or in the production process (P); (c) information for safety (I).

requirement: Tailoring the usability engineering effort
The extent to which each subclause of Clause 5 is applied shall be appropriate to the device, considering its intended use, primary operating functions, severity and probability of potential harm.
Usability Engineering Process
Prepare Use Specification

The manufacturer shall identify and document the following characteristics: (a) intended medical indication; (b) intended patient population; (c) intended part of the body or type of tissue applied to or interacted with; (d) intended user profile; (e) use environment; (f) operating principle.
Identify user interface characteristics related to safety and potential use errors
The manufacturer shall identify user interface characteristics that could be related to safety as part of the use specification.
Identify known or foreseeable hazard and hazardous situations
The manufacturer shall identify known or foreseeable hazards and hazardous situations, which could arise as a result of use errors.
Identify and describe hazards-related use scenarios
The manufacturer shall identify and describe the reasonably foreseeable hazards-related use scenarios.
Select the hazards-related use scenarios for summative evaluation
The manufacturer shall select the hazard-related use scenarios to be included in the summative evaluation.
Establish user interface specification
The manufacturer shall establish and document a user interface specification, including the test criteria.
Establish user interface evaluation plan
The manufacturer shall establish and document a user interface evaluation plan.
Perform user interface design implementation and formative evaluation
The manufacturer shall implement the user interface design and perform formative evaluations as appropriate during the design.
Perform summative evaluation of the usability of the user interface

The manufacturer shall perform a summative evaluation of the usability of the user interface to demonstrate that the device can be used safely by the intended user(s) for the intended uses in the intended use environment.
